title_en: GRAS: The Loophole in Your Food
title_es: GRAS: La Laguna en Tu Comida
hook_en: Did you know companies can add new chemicals to food without telling the FDA?
hook_es: ¿Sabías que las empresas pueden añadir químicos nuevos a los alimentos sin informar al FDA?
key_message: The GRAS self-affirmation process allows companies to add new chemicals to food without FDA notification, posing potential risks to consumers.
visual_elements: Close-up of a food label with unrecognizable ingredients
Quick cut to a shocked expression
Chart showing the rise in obesity rates alongside introduction of GRAS
Side-by-side comparison of foods with and without additives
_source_regulation_context:
KEY REGULATORY EVENTS:
  • 1996: GRAS self-affirmation process formalized — Impact: Companies no longer need to notify FDA before adding new chemicals to food

US OBESITY RATES OVER TIME:
  • 1960: 13.4% (adults), 5.0% (children)
  • 1980: 15.0% (adults), 5.5% (children)
  • 1994: 22.5% (adults), 10.0% (children)
  • 2000: 30.5% (adults), 13.9% (children)
  • 2010: 35.7% (adults), 16.9% (children)
  • 2016: 39.8% (adults), 18.5% (children)
  • 2020: 41.9% (adults), 19.7% (children)
  • 2024: 43.0% (adults), 21.0% (children)

title_en: Why Your Snacks Have Chemicals Banned in Europe
title_es: Por qué Tus Bocadillos Tienen Químicos Prohibidos en Europa
hook_en: Ever wondered why some snacks in the US contain chemicals banned in Europe?
hook_es: ¿Te has preguntado por qué algunos bocadillos en EE.UU. tienen químicos prohibidos en Europa?
key_message: US food regulations allow additives banned in Europe due to health concerns, impacting public health.
visual_elements: Comparison of US and European snack ingredient lists
Zoom in on specific banned additives
Animation of a globe highlighting US and Europe
Facial reaction of disgust while holding a snack
props_needed: snack packages from US and Europe
